content:
IMO this is a suitable Reverse clash; pattern recognition was clear and simple to spot. Feel free to enable Fastest/Shortest modes if you want :)

Some preliminary comments from me:

- Suggestion: include test cases where length and/or characters is 0? Not sure how suitable that would be for this clash.

- Please add some test cases where the required number of characters is a multiple of 8 and is not equal to the length of the string.

- To balance out the number of test cases where the output has trailing Xs in the bottom left corner of the grid, please add / modify some test cases to have trailing Xs in the bottom right corner of the grid.

Also for reviewers: there exists a related but not too similar clash: https://www.codingame.com/contribute/view/126504864e7133e8ebd16e670b7e00f3797c70

This is temporarily a Reverse puzzle only just so I can see if the pattern is too easy or too hard to spot. Both beginners and veterans are welcome to give it a try, and if you have time, please give me feedback on the pattern recognition speed. Was it instant, did it take 0-3 minutes, did it take 3-7 minutes, or could you not recognize it at all. Once I've gotten a good idea of how this puzzle might do in Reverse, then I'll enable Fastest, add a statement, and decide whether to keep it or not.

Also if I'm missing any edge cases (if you can even spot any), feel free to alert me about it, and I'll happily take a look at it.

(and most importantly, PLEASE DO NOT DOWNVOTE JUST BECAUSE REVERSE MIGHT BE TOO HARD; I will add a statement later, don't worry :)
Do you want to draw reviewers' attention that the stub generator contains a hint?
Oh good point! I'll remove those hints for the meantime to test the strength of the pattern, but if the pattern is impossible to spot for some players, then I'll add them back.
Well, there are also the clash title and the test case titles to consider, if you're going for a completely hint-free experience.
